{{Quote|No one orders the Jedi. We accept requests. Then we decide.|Ry-Gaul|<ref name="TDG">''[[Jedi Quest: The Dangerous Games]]''</ref>}}
'''Ry-Gaul''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] during the twilight years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Serving the Republic as a [[Senior Jedi General|Senior]] [[Jedi General/Legends|General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], Ry-Gaul fought in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]]. When the Order was disbanded and the Republic fell to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Master Ry-Gaul went to ground; eventually falling victim to the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].

==Biography==
===Jedi Master===
{{Quote|There is less to say.|Ry-Gaul, to [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]|<ref name="TFS">''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]''</ref>}}
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]], Ry-Gaul was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] at the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]; eventually the man achieved the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Tall and imposing, Master Ry-Gaul was widely respected by his peers, considered to be very knowledgeable about [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. A silent man, Master Ry-Gaul only spoke when he needed too, demonstrative of his deeply thoughtful nature. Having constructed a silver-bladed [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] during his time as a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], Master Ry-Gaul selected the young [[Teevan/Legends|Teevan]] [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiate]] [[Tru Veld/Legends|Tru Veld]] to be his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] around [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].<ref name="WOTA" />

During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], which plagued the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], Master Ry-Gaul and Padawan Veld were sent on many missions, keeping the peace during the [[Galactic Games/Legends|Galactic Games]] on [[Euceron]] and [[Mission to Radnor|ending violence]] on [[Radnor/Legends|Radnor]] among many others.<ref name="TDG" /><ref name="TMOT">''[[Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth]]''</ref> In [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], Master and apprentice accompanied Jedi [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]] and [[Soara Antana/Legends|Soara Antana]], along with Padawans [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], [[Ferus Olin]] and [[Darra Thel-Tanis]] on a dangerous mission to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] tombworld of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. During [[Mission to Korriban (23 BBY)|this mission]], the aim of which was to track down and apprehend [[Sith cultist/Legends|Sith Cultist]] [[Granta Omega]] and mad [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Jenna Zan Arbor]], Padawan Veld's lightsaber malfunctioned, leading to the death of Thel-Tanis.<ref name="TFS" />

===Clone Wars===
When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] erupted between the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] and the Republic, Master Ry-Gaul took up the rank of [[Senior Jedi General|Senior General]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] and led [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Second Army|2nd Sector Army]] against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]].<ref name="Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic">{{InsiderCite|84|Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic}}</ref>

In the last months of the war, [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] personally dispatched Master Ry-Gaul on a highly secret mission to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. In his absence, he tasked his former Padawan and friend Jedi Veld to research weaponry in the Jedi Temple [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Archives]], thinking that he would be safe there. During Master Ry-Gaul's mission, the [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the Republic, [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], revealed his true intentions to the Order and issued [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|Contingency]] [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] to the Grand Army, which commanded the clones to turn on their Jedi allies and execute them. With the Order effectively disbanded, the Republic collapsed and was replaced by a Sith-run [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="ATE">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire]]''</ref>

===Exile===
Because he was still undercover, Master Ry-Gaul was left unaware of the events transpiring around the galaxy. However, he was intercepted by two [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]], [[Tobin Gantor]] and [[Linna Naltree]], who assisted him in evading detection by the Empire by giving him false IDdocs and helped him return to Coruscant in search of answers. Learning that Tru Veld had died on Coruscant during the attack which left the Temple in ruin, Master Ry-Gaul delved deep into the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant underworld]] where he met with Master [[Fy-Tor-Ana]], now known as Solace. Master Ana had been tracking him, searching him out to warn him that Imperial Grand [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Hydra (Grand Inquisitor)|Hydra]] was also on the hunt for the former Senior General.<ref name="ATE" />

During his time on Coruscant, Master Ry-Gaul had begun to discover a trail of missing scientists from around the galaxy, including his saviors, Gantor and Naltree. In the company of Master Ana and her companion, former Jedi [[Ferus Olin]], Master Ry-Gaul met the young boy named [[Lune Oddo Divinian|Lune Divinian]]. Agreeing to help train the young Force-sensitive boy, the task soon became too overwhelming for the man, who had more pressing matters, and Divinian was sent to Olin's [[Jedi asteroid|hidden asteroid base]] to train with Master [[Garen Muln]].<ref name="ATE" />

Joined by Ferus Olin's young friend [[Trever Flume]], Master Ry-Gaul set out on a mission to rescue Linna Naltree from the clutches of the crazed scientist [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]. Infiltrating her [[Jenna Zan Arbor's apartment|apartment]] on Coruscant, the Jedi Master and Flume were forced to go on the defensive as they escorted Naltree to a [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]]. Drawing his lightsaber as Zan Arbor and her security team closed in, Naltree injected Zan Arbor with the madwoman's own [[Selective memory erasing drug|memory-erasing drug]], wiping clean Zan Arbor's twisted, yet brilliant mind.<ref name="MOD">''[[The Last of the Jedi: Master of Deception]]''</ref>

His mission complete, Master Ry-Gaul and his team of associates and allies were met with the dilemma of escaping [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]]. Attending the [[Galactic Luxury Starship Manufacturers Convention]] in a bid to commandeer a ride, Master Ry-Gaul used the [[Jedi Mind Tricks|Force to persuade]] an executive of [[Sleeker Systems]] to sell a protype of a new model. Soon after departing, the team noted a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] attached to the ship; this discovery eventually allowed the group to uncover a mole amongst their number. Rebel leader [[Eve Yarrow|Flame]], who had worked with the group on many assignments, was revealed to be the Imperial agent Eve Yarrow. Acting decisively, Ry-Gaul ditched the ship in a remote spaceport while Master Ana left Yarrow on a remote world, the Jedi team was able to regroup back at the hidden asteroid base established by Ferus Olin.<ref name="MOD" />

===Death===
Despite their struggles and tribulations, the respite and solace that the hidden base promised proved false. Another turncoat had penetrated the group and all were betrayed as [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] moved to destroy the base with his new [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. When the group attempted to flee, their remaining starfighters exploded, sabotaged by the traitor on the base. Stranded, Masters Ry-Gaul, Ana, and Muln gathered the young acolyte Lune Divinian inside an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] and saw him safely off the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]].<ref name="LOTJR" />

The three Jedi, colleagues from a dead era, stood side by side as they realized and accepted that the odds were insurmountable. Vader's superweapon was fired once, and the asteroid was destroyed utterly, killing all those present on its surface.<ref name="LOTJR" />

==Personality and traits==
Ry-Gaul was known as a tall, dignified, and quiet man who seldom spoke. However, when he did speak, it was usually worth listening to, for his wisdom and experience made him widely respected.<ref name="WOTA" />

==Powers and abilities==
Ry-Gaul was a powerful [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combatant]], but often prevented fights through simple intimidation. His technique, according to Obi-Wan Kenobi, mimicked [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn's]] version of [[Form IV/Legends|Ataru]]. Kenobi further observed that, much like Jinn had, Ry-Gaul moved quickly for his size, his grace surprising while his great strength never flagged.<ref name="TFS" />
